raw_title: Addressing Media Harm and Community Relationships through an Ethic of Love
raw_abstract: Ethical approaches to address media harm and build more idealized relationships between journalists and marginalized communities have relied heavily on prescriptions for unidirectional journalistic practices, often framed as an ethic of care . In this exploration of relationship development and maintenance through the perspectives of trusted journalists, we identify practices and exchanges that reflect a more reciprocal framework of an ethic of love . We present a case study derived from interviews with seventeen journalists named as trusted by Black community members in Minneapolis, Minnesota, where police murdered George Floyd in 2020. His murder reignited an international movement to address police brutality and systemic racism. Results show that communities and journalists rely on each other to achieve healthy relationships and exchanges. These relationships require both mutual agency and reciprocity, and many dimensions of their exchanges align with Black feminist scholar bell hooks’ ethic of love. Trusted journalists successfully addressed historic media harm through two prominent practices identified in this study: diligence and deference. Findings call upon scholars to craft a more inclusive and internationally relevant understanding of journalistic strategies that repair historic harms by centering vulnerable communities—for the ultimate benefit of all communities. Drawing on design justice principles, which posit that centering the most vulnerable communities creates more inclusive and universal systems, we discuss how the ethic of love framework can strengthen information pathways between journalists and communities.
title_en: Addressing Media Harm and Community Relationships through an Ethic of Love
title_zh: 通过爱的伦理应对媒体伤害与社区关系
abstract_en: Ethical approaches to address media harm and build more idealized relationships between journalists and marginalized communities have relied heavily on prescriptions for unidirectional journalistic practices, often framed as an ethic of care. In this exploration of relationship development and maintenance through the perspectives of trusted journalists, we identify practices and exchanges that reflect a more reciprocal framework of an ethic of love. We present a case study derived from interviews with seventeen journalists named as trusted by Black community members in Minneapolis, Minnesota, where police murdered George Floyd in 2020. His murder reignited an international movement to address police brutality and systemic racism. Results show that communities and journalists rely on each other to achieve healthy relationships and exchanges. These relationships require both mutual agency and reciprocity, and many dimensions of their exchanges align with Black feminist scholar bell hooks’ ethic of love. Trusted journalists successfully addressed historic media harm through two prominent practices identified in this study: diligence and deference. Findings call upon scholars to craft a more inclusive and internationally relevant understanding of journalistic strategies that repair historic harms by centering vulnerable communities—for the ultimate benefit of all communities. Drawing on design justice principles, which posit that centering the most vulnerable communities creates more inclusive and universal systems, we discuss how the ethic of love framework can strengthen information pathways between journalists and communities.
abstract_zh: 解决媒体伤害并建立记者与边缘化社区之间更理想关系的伦理方法，在很大程度上依赖于单向新闻实践的规范，通常被框架为关怀伦理。在这项通过受信任记者视角探索关系发展和维护的研究中，我们识别出反映更互惠的爱的伦理框架的实践和交流。我们呈现了一个案例研究，该研究基于对明尼苏达州明尼阿波利斯市被黑人社区成员视为可信赖的17名记者的访谈，2020年警察在那里谋杀了乔治·弗洛伊德。他的谋杀重新点燃了解决警察暴行和系统性种族主义的国际运动。结果表明，社区和记者相互依赖以实现健康的关系和交流。这些关系需要相互代理和互惠，他们交流的许多维度与黑人女性主义学者贝尔·胡克斯的爱的伦理一致。受信任的记者通过本研究中确定的两个突出实践成功解决了历史性的媒体伤害：勤奋和尊重。研究结果呼吁学者们构建一个更具包容性和国际相关性的新闻策略理解，通过以弱势社区为中心来修复历史伤害——最终惠及所有社区。借鉴设计正义原则，该原则认为以最脆弱的社区为中心可以创造更具包容性和普遍性的系统，我们讨论了爱的伦理框架如何加强记者与社区之间的信息路径。
plain_summary_zh: 这项研究探讨了记者如何与黑人社区建立信任关系，以修复媒体造成的伤害。通过对明尼阿波利斯市17名受信任记者的访谈，研究者发现，记者和社区之间需要相互尊重和合作，这种关系可以用“爱的伦理”来描述。记者通过勤奋和尊重两种方式，成功改善了与社区的关系。研究建议，新闻业应该更多地关注弱势群体的需求，这样对所有社区都有好处。
plain_summary_en: This study explores how journalists build trust with Black communities to repair media harm. Through interviews with 17 trusted journalists in Minneapolis, researchers found that mutual respect and cooperation between journalists and communities can be described as an 'ethic of love.' Journalists successfully improved relationships through diligence and deference. The study suggests that journalism should focus more on vulnerable communities, benefiting everyone.
research_direction_zh: 新闻伦理与社区关系修复
research_direction_en: Journalism ethics and community relationship repair
